config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or by the state. In Indonesia, I have seen how easy it is for “public interest” to be used as a slogan to silence dissent or ignore minority voices. A healthy society should balance both, but the burden should be high before overriding individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, that idea makes me wary because history shows how often "the common good" has been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Society absolutely has legitimate needs like public safety, disease control, and fair laws, but those should be narrow exceptions with clear limits, not a blanket excuse to override rights. In my view, a society that casually sacrifices individual rights is not healthy or free, even if it claims to be acting for the greater good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “public interest” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse unfair treatment, so I am very skeptical of any blanket claim that society comes first. A society is healthier when it protects individual rights strongly and only limits them in truly necessary, narrowly defined cases like public safety or preventing direct harm. If we reverse that order too easily, we give too much power to the majority and too much room for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not take automatic priority over individual rights. From a United States perspective, I am very suspicious of any blanket claim that “society” comes first, because that has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach in the name of order or the common good. I do think there are real exceptions, like public health, criminal law, and emergency safety measures, but those need narrow limits and strong justification. If society can override rights too easily, then the rights are not really rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society’s interests should matter, but I do not accept that they should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In Indonesia, I have seen how “public interest” can become a convenient label for suppressing criticism, pressuring minorities, or excusing unfair treatment, so I am very cautious when people ask individuals to sacrifice too much for the collective. Yes, there are real exceptions like public safety and disease control, but those limits must be narrow, justified, and temporary, or else the majority will always have too much power. If individual rights can be overridden whenever it is politically convenient, then those rights are not meaningful protections anymore.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, and I do not trust “the common good” when it is defined by the people already in power. Society has real needs, but they should justify only narrow, clearly limited exceptions like public safety or public health, not a broad license to override rights. If individual rights can be brushed aside whenever it is convenient, then they are not real rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
